description_long: This is a metallic adjustable wrench, commonly used for gripping and turning nuts and bolts of various sizes. The tool features a robust, elongated handle with a hole at the end, likely for hanging or storage. The head of the wrench has a movable lower jaw, which can be adjusted using a knurled worm screw mechanism visible on the side. The jaws are flat and parallel, designed to provide a firm grip on fasteners without damaging them. The surface of the wrench is smooth with a matte metallic finish, and there are no visible rubber or plastic inserts. The overall appearance suggests durability and suitability for heavy-duty tasks in urban maintenance or repair settings.
description: A metallic adjustable wrench with a smooth, matte finish and a movable jaw for gripping fasteners. The handle is elongated and features a hanging hole at the end.
description_view_0: The wrench is shown from the side, clearly displaying the adjustable jaw, worm screw, and the full length of the handle.
description_view_1: A narrow edge-on view, highlighting the thin profile of the handle and the head, with the hanging hole visible at the end.
description_view_2: The opposite side view, showing the jaw opening and the worm screw mechanism, with the handle extending back.
description_view_3: An angled perspective focusing on the head and jaw, emphasizing the thickness and construction of the wrench.
